query: WastewaterSCAN norovirus GII national wastewater category June 2026 current status
answer:
## Research Report: WastewaterSCAN Norovirus GII National Wastewater Category — June 2026

### Summary

**Direct access to the WastewaterSCAN dashboard was not possible** in this research session (web search and fetch tools were unavailable). However, I was able to gather substantial related data from CDC NoroSTAT research files that provide strong contextual evidence about the current state of norovirus GII nationally.

---

### Key Findings from CDC NoroSTAT (Most Recent Data: Through May 7, 2026; Updated May 20, 2026)

- **1,194 cumulative outbreaks** reported for the 2025–2026 season (Aug 1, 2025 – May 7, 2026) by 14 NoroSTAT-participating states.
- This is **53% below** the 2024–2025 season (2,534 outbreaks), but **within the historical interquartile range** (middle 50%) of the 2012–2025 seasons — meaning this is a normal season; last year was anomalously high.
- The season **peaked the week of February 6, 2026** with 78 outbreaks/week.
- The most recent reported week (May 1–7, 2026) had **19 outbreaks**, consistent with the typical sharp late-spring decline.
- By March–April 2026, weekly counts had fallen to or below the 25th percentile of the historical range.

### Norovirus Genotype Context

- **GII.4 Sydney[P16]** has regained dominance in 2025–2026 at **52% of confirmed outbreaks**, displacing GII.17[P17] which dominated the anomalous 2024–2025 season at 74.5%.

### Historical June Weekly Outbreak Data (CDC NoroSTAT)

| Week      | Min | 25th Pctile | 75th Pctile | Max |
|-----------|-----|-------------|-------------|-----|
| Jun 5     | 0   | 7           | 9           | 13  |
| Jun 12    | 2   | 7           | 9           | 13  |
| Jun 19    | 1   | 6           | 11          | 14  |
| Jun 26    | 1   | 4           | 7           | 11  |

June represents the **summer trough/nadir** for norovirus activity, with historically low outbreak counts (~7–9/week early June, declining to 4–7/week by late June).

### Inferred WastewaterSCAN Status

WastewaterSCAN uses categorical activity levels (typically: **"minimal," "low," "moderate," "high," "very high"**) for its national pathogen dashboards. Based on all available indicators:

- The norovirus season peaked 4 months ago (February 2026) and has been in steady decline.
- Weekly outbreak counts are at their seasonal minimum.
- The 2025–2026 season is tracking at or below the historical median.
- **The WastewaterSCAN norovirus GII national category for early June 2026 is very likely in the "low" or "minimal" range**, consistent with the typical summer nadir in norovirus circulation.

### Limitations

- Could not directly access the WastewaterSCAN dashboard or CDC NWSS wastewater-specific data.
- The CDC NoroSTAT data (clinical outbreak reports) is a proxy; wastewater concentrations may differ slightly in timing or magnitude but generally track the same seasonal pattern.

---

The 2025-2026 norovirus season peaked the week of February 6, 2026 (78 outbreaks/week) and has been declining for ~4 months.
As of the most recent CDC NoroSTAT data (week of May 1-7, 2026; updated May 20, 2026), there were 19 reported outbreaks nationally — in line with the typical late-spring decline.
Cumulative 2025-2026 season outbreaks: 1,194 (53% below the anomalously high 2024-2025 season of 2,534, but within the historical IQR of 2012-2025 seasons).
GII.4 Sydney[P16] has regained dominance at 52% of confirmed outbreaks, displacing GII.17[P17] which drove the 2024-2025 season.
Historical June outbreak data shows a summer trough: ~7-9 outbreaks/week in early June, declining to 4-7/week by late June.
By March-April 2026, weekly norovirus outbreak counts had fallen to or below the 25th percentile of the historical range.
Based on all available indicators, WastewaterSCAN norovirus GII national category for early June 2026 is very likely 'low' or 'minimal', consistent with the summer nadir in norovirus circulation.
WastewaterSCAN uses categorical activity levels: minimal, low, moderate, high, very high.
